title: Our First Classes
summary:
• Understand how to define a reference type in a Java class.
  • Understand a Java class/type consists of instance variables, constructors, and instance methods.
  • Understand how to create a tester class to test our type.
  • Understand how to declare variables of reference types.
  • Understand how to allocate objects by using new and constructors.
  • Understand how to manipulate objects by calling instance methods.
  • Understand that objects are allocated in heap memory.
  • Undertand that a reference variable has enough memory (4-bytes) allocated to hold the refence to the object allocated in heap memory.
  • Understand the naming convention for Java types.

title: Java Random
summary:
• Understand Java Random
  • Understand the details of declaring variables, allocating objects, and using objects of type Random.
  • Understand Java Random has a set of values and a set of operations.

  • Understand the differences between Scanner and String.

title: Simple Objects
summary:
The learning objectives for this entire module.
  • Understance objects in the real-world.
  • Understand software objects have state and behavior.
  • Understand that Java String, Scanner, and Random are reference types - not primitive types.
  • Understand the details of declaring variables, allocating objects, and using objects of type String, Scanner, and Random.
  • Understand an object's state and behavior are like a data type's set of values and set of operations.
  • Understand how to define a reference type in a Java class.
  • Understand a Java class/type consists of instance variables, constructors, and instance methods.
  • Understand how to create a tester class to test our type.
  • Understand how to declare variables of reference types.
  • Understand how to allocate objects by using new and constructors.
  • Understand how to manipulate objects by calling instance methods.
  • Understand that objects are allocated in heap memory.
  • Undertand that a reference variable has enough memory (4-bytes) allocated to hold the refence to the object allocated in heap memory.
  • Understand the naming convention for Java types.
  • Understand JavaDoc.
  • Understand how to include JavaDoc in your classes.

title: Java Strings
summary:
• Understand Java Strings
  • Understand Java Strings are a reference type - not a primitive type.
  • Understand the details of declaring variables, allocating objects, and using objects of type String.
  • Understand instance methods.
  • Understand how to call String instance methods

title: Conditional Statements
summary:
• Understand Java's conditional statements
  • Understand control flow associated with if and switch
  • Understand one-way, two-way, and multi-way ifs.
  • Understand ifs require a boolean expression.
  • Understand a dangling else.
  • Understand switches require expressions that evaluate to int, short, byte, char, String, or an enum type.
  • Understand the use of break within a switch
  • Understand how to create equivalent if and switch statements

title: Loops
keywords: control flow, loop, for loop, while loop, do-while loop
summary:
• Understand Java loops.
  • Understand loops iterate.
  • Understand the body of loops can be a single statement or a block
  • Understand while loops
  • Understand that all problems can be solved with the while loop - the for and do-while can be done with while
  • Understand while loops may iterate zero times.
  • Understand while loops may interate infinitely.
  • Understand for loops
  • Understand a for loop can be accomplished with a while.
  • Understand a counting for loop.
  • Understand a for loop iteration variable
  • Understand how to use a counting for loop to iterate through Strings.
  • Understand for loop expressions do not have to be counting expressions.
  • Understande do-while loops.
  • Understand a do-while loop iterates at least one time.

  • Understand final and its use on declaring variables.
  • Understand how to define an enumeration type.
  • Understand how to use an enumeration type.
  • Understand subclasses.
  • Understand how to create a Student that is a subclass of a Person.
  • Understand class hierarchy.
  • Understand a Java interface.
  • Understand a Java interface contains method signatures, but does not contain code.
  • Understand how to write a class that implements a Java interface.
  • Understand overriding toString and comparable.

  • Understand String variables cannot be modified when passes as an actual parameter.
  • Understand that Java is pass by value.
  • Understand methods return values.

title: ArrayLists
summary:
• Understand ArrayLists are a sequential collection of data.
  • Understand each element of an ArrayList is the same type.
  • Understand elements of an ArrayList have indices from 0 to the ArrayList.size()-1.
  • Understand how to declare ArrayList variables.
  • Understand how to allocate ArrayList objects.
  • Understand the relationship between ArrayList variables, ArrayList objects, and memory.
  • Understand how to use ArrayList methods to access ArrayList elements.
  • Understand that ArrayList elements must be reference types.
  • Understand how primitive types are autoboxed into reference types.
  • Understand the for-each loop and how to use it with ArrayList.

title: Arrays
summary:
• Understand arrays are a sequential collection of data.
  • Understand each element of an array is the same type.
  • Understand elements of an array have indices from 0 to the array.length-1.
  • Understand how to declare array variables.
  • Understand how to allocate array objects.
  • Understand the relationship between array variable, array objects, and memory.
  • Understand how to use array syntax of [] to access array elements.
  • Understand the for-each loop and how to use it with arrays.
  • Understand how to pass arrays as parameters.
  • Understand how to reallcoate an array.
  • Understand the main parameter String[] args.

title: Arrays and ArrayLists
summary:
• Understand arrays and ArrayLists are a sequential collections of data.
  • Understand each element of an arrays and ArrayList is the same type.
  • Understand elements of an arrays and ArrayList have indices from 0 to their sizes-1
  • Understand how to declare arrays and ArrayList variables.
  • Understand how to allocate arrays and ArrayList objects.
  • Understand the relationship between arrays and ArrayList variables, objects, and memory.
  • Understand similarities and differences between arrays and ArrayLists.

title: Packages
summary:
• Understand Java packages.
  • Understand how to create Java packages.
  • Understand how to import Java packages.

title: Packages, ADTs
summary:
The learning objectives for this entire module.
  • Understand Java packages.
  • Understand how to create Java packages.
  • Understand how to import Java packages.
  • Understand Abstract Data Types.
  • Understand and implement a queue ADT.
  • Understand and implement a stack ADT.
  • Use an interface when implementing a queue and a stack.
  • Explore Java generic types for queues and stacks.
  • Understand how to implement your own exception.
  • Solve problems with a queue and a stack.

title: Searching
summary:
• Understand searching.
  • Understand linear search and its associated Big O value.
  • Understand binary search and its associated Big O value.
  • Understand hash tables and how they are related to searching.
  • Understand the String.hashCode method.
  • Understand Java's key-value data structures Map and HashMap.

title: Sorting
summary:
• Understand sorting sequential collections arrays and ArrayLists.
  • Understand bubble sort and its Big O value.
  • Understand selection sort and its Big O value.
  • Understand merge sort and its Big O value.

title: Battleship
summary:
• Understand Java interfaces.
  • Understand Java packages.
  • Understand how to retain state between calls of methods.
  • Understand how to use char[] for implementing game boards.

title: Your Choice
summary: Your Choice

title: Cipher
summary:
• Understand elementary encryption algorithms
  • Think about encryption as it relates to events in life such as the Internet.
  • Understand ASCII and UTF encodings of char.
  • Understand the char type can be used in arithmetic expressions.
  • Practice writing Java classes and creating objects.
  • Practice writing a Java tester class.
  • Practice writing JavaDoc.
  • Begin to think about Project 5 - Your Choice.

title: Endor Smints
summary:
• Practice writing a simulation game.
  • Understand how to maintain state within a class.
  • Understand how to incrementally develop a program.
  • Understand how to develop test cases prior to developiong code.
  • Have fun creating the EndorSmints game.

title: Shared Secret
summary:
• Understand modulo arithmetic.
  • Understand the basics of public-private keys.
